process.heading: From dream to cake in four steps.

01.title: Conversation
01.description: Tell us about your event, theme, and guest count via the form or WhatsApp.

02.title: Briefing
02.description: Our team gets in touch to clarify details, palette, and references.

03.title: Creation
03.description: You'll receive a custom project—we approve every detail together before production.

04.title: Delivery
04.description: We bake 7 to 30 days ahead (depending on size) and deliver to your venue.

fields.messagePlaceholder: Theme, colour palette, references, dietary restrictions...
fields.lgpd: I agree to be contacted about my quote.

success.title: We've received your request
success.body: We'll be in touch shortly. Thank you for trusting Carlo's Bakery.

errors.phone: Please enter a valid WhatsApp number.
errors.eventDateMin: The date must be at least 14 days from today.
errors.guests: Please select the number of guests.

leadTime.question: How far in advance should I enquire?
leadTime.answer: We recommend at least 14 days for simpler cakes and 30+ days for sculptural or wedding cakes.

pricing.question: What's the typical investment?
pricing.answer: Custom cakes start at R$800. Pricing depends on complexity, size, and theme.

delivery.question: Do you deliver to the venue?
delivery.answer: Yes—in São Paulo and the metropolitan region. Shipping rates apply. Pickup at our Vila Leopoldina factory is free.

tasting.question: Can I taste before I order?
tasting.answer: Yes—we offer tastings by appointment at our shop.

dietary.question: Do you accommodate dietary restrictions?
dietary.answer: Yes—we offer gluten-free, dairy-free, and vegan options. Please note them on the form.

payment.question: How does payment work?
payment.answer: 50% to start production and the remaining 50% up to 7 days before the event.

finalCta.heading: Ready to design your cake?
finalCta.subhead: Count on 115 years of Italian tradition for life's biggest moments.

meta.title: Our Story | Carlo's Bakery São Paulo
meta.description: From Hoboken to São Paulo: four generations of the Valastro family, from Carlo Guastaffero to Buddy Valastro, the Cake Boss.

chapter1.headline: It all began with an Italian dream.
chapter1.subhead: Hoboken, New Jersey, 1910. A baker named Carlo Guastaffero opens the doors of a small bakery at 95 Washington Street. One hundred and fifteen years later, his recipe crosses the ocean.

chapter2.heading: Carlo.
chapter2.imageAlt: Historic Carlo's Bakery storefront in Hoboken, New Jersey
chapter2.caption: The bakery on Washington Street — Hoboken, since 1910.
paragraphs: Carlo Guastaffero arrived in the United States from Altamura, in southern Italy, carrying his family's recipes and the determination of someone who crosses an ocean in search of a new life.

In the heart of Hoboken, New Jersey, he opened a small bakery that quickly became a gathering place for the Italian immigrant community. Cannoli, puff pastry, amaretti cookies — all made as in the old country.
chapter2.quote: Carlo used to say: "The pastry shop is the soul of a family."

chapter3.heading: A lineage of masters.
chapter3.subhead: From Carlo to Buddy, each generation added a chapter to this story.

e1910.title: Carlo opens the bakery
e1910.description: The original storefront on Washington Street in Hoboken — where it all began.

e1964.title: Bartolo takes over
e1964.description: Bartolo Valastro Sr., future Buddy's stepfather, buys the bakery and renames it Carlo's Bakery.

e1990.title: Buddy learns from his father
e1990.description: In the bakery kitchen, father and son shape dough by hand — tradition passed from one generation to the next.

e1994.title: Buddy takes over at 17
e1994.description: In his Carlo's apron, Buddy already creates towering wedding cakes that set the house standard.

e2009.title: Cake Boss is born
e2009.description: TLC premieres the reality show that would make Buddy a global star. More than 200 episodes across 12 seasons.

e2015.title: Buddy in Brazil
e2015.description: The first Carlo's Bakery outside the United States opens in São Paulo. Still the only one in Brazil today.

eToday.title: Living tradition
eToday.description: Buddy and the Valastro family in the kitchen — four generations of bakers, one story.

chapter4.heading: Buddy.
chapter4.imageAlt: Portrait of Buddy Valastro, Carlo's Bakery pastry chef
paragraphs: At 17, Buddy inherited a bakery on the brink of bankruptcy and the mission to honor his father's legacy. Within a decade, he turned Carlo's Bakery into a world reference in artisan pastry.

In 2009, TLC premiered "Cake Boss," the reality show that captured daily life at the bakery. The series became a global phenomenon, aired in more than 100 countries. Buddy became the world's most famous pastry chef.
But for him, the story was never about fame. It was always about family.
chapter4.quote: "Family means everything. Everything you do, you do for your family."

chapter5.heading: A global phenomenon.
chapter5.subhead: The Carlo's legacy grew beyond Hoboken — onto screens and display cases around the world.

milestones.episodes: Cake Boss episodes aired on television
milestones.countries: countries where the show was broadcast

chapter6.heading: From Hoboken to São Paulo.
chapter6.subhead: In 2015, after decades of requests from Brazilian fans, Buddy Valastro chose São Paulo to open the first (and only) Carlo's Bakery outside the United States. It wasn't chance. It was destiny.

chapter7.heading: Carlo's in Brazil.
paragraphs: For ten years, we have kept the Valastro family tradition alive in São Paulo. Every cannoli, every lobster tail, every sculptural cake carries the same soul Carlo Guastaffero put into his bakery in 1910.
More than a franchise, we are guardians of a legacy. And the only address where, outside the United States, you can taste the real Carlo's.

galleryAlt.0: Carlo's Bakery storefront display in São Paulo

kherlakian.heading: The Kherlakian Family
kherlakian.body: Just as the Valastro family runs Carlo's Bakery in the United States, the Kherlakian family was chosen by Buddy to care for his stores in Brazil. The whole family is closely involved in day-to-day operations and commercial strategy to ensure Carlo's Bakery succeeds in the country.
kherlakian.imageAlt: The Kherlakian family — Marcos, Milah, Marcos Filho and Mariana, who lead Carlo's Bakery Brazil

chapter8.headline: One hundred and fifteen years later, it's still family.
chapter8.subhead: Every visit to Carlo's is another chapter in this story. Be part of it.

free.answer: Yes, 100% free. No annual fee, no charge. You only earn.

earn.question: How do I earn points?
earn.answer: Every R$ 1 spent equals 1 point. Valid for store purchases and iFood orders when linked to the app.

expire.question: Do points expire?
expire.answer: Points are valid for 12 months from the earning date. They renew with every new purchase.

stores.question: Can I use it at any store?
stores.answer: Yes. All 8 Carlo's Bakery locations accept the program. Just show the app QR code at checkout.

tier.question: How do I change tiers?
tier.answer: Automatically. When you reach the required points, the app notifies you and the upgrade happens within 24h.

transfer.question: Can I transfer points?
transfer.answer: Points are not transferable between accounts, but you can create a voucher to gift someone.

phone.question: What if I lose my phone?
phone.answer: No problem. Your points are linked to your CPF/email. Just download the app again and log in.

final.heading: Ready to begin?
final.subhead: Join +50,000 members and turn every Carlo's visit into a reward.
final.guarantee: Sign up in 30 seconds · No credit card

hero.headline: Talk to the family.
hero.subhead: Press, partnerships, careers, support or just to say hello — reach us on WhatsApp and our team will reply quickly.

departments.heading: How can we help?
departments.subhead: Four departments. Replies within one business day.

press.title: Press
press.description: Stories, Buddy Valastro interviews and media materials.
press.subject: Press request

partnerships.title: Corporate Partnerships
partnerships.description: Corporate events, branded gifts and brand collaborations.
partnerships.subject: Partnership proposal

careers.title: Careers
careers.description: Join the Carlo's Bakery Brazil family. Send your resume.
careers.subject: Resume - Carlo's Bakery

support.title: Customer Support
support.description: Questions about orders, suggestions and complaints.
support.subject: Customer support

fields.subject: Subject
fields.lgpd: I agree to be contacted by Carlo's Bakery about my message.

success.title: Message sent
success.body: We received your message. Our team replies within one business day.

errors.subject: Select a subject.
errors.message: Write a message with at least 10 characters.

direct.heading: Prefer something more direct?
direct.hoursWeek: Monday to Saturday · 10 AM to 10 PM

faq.heading: Your question may already have an answer.

customCake.question: How do I order a custom cake?
customCake.answer: Message us on WhatsApp or visit our Vila Leopoldina store. Our team replies within one business day.

ifood.question: Do you deliver through iFood?
ifood.answer: Yes, all 8 locations are on iFood. Search for 'Carlo's Bakery' in the app.

outsideSp.question: Can I order outside São Paulo?
outsideSp.answer: We serve the São Paulo metro area. For other cities, contact us by email.

franchise.question: I want to open a franchise. How does it work?
franchise.answer: Contact us on WhatsApp with your profile and city of interest.

supplier.question: How can I become a supplier?
supplier.answer: Send your proposal on WhatsApp with portfolio and company information.

final.heading: Didn't find what you needed?
final.subhead: Message us on WhatsApp. Human support, fast reply.
